red.label: High risk / non-compliant
criteria: Material legal, financial, operational, or breach exposure
No reliable evidence, or evidence is ad hoc/outdated

yellow.label: Partial / immature
criteria: Control exists but is incomplete, inconsistent, or not enforced everywhere
Evidence exists but is manual, partial, or not recent
Process depends on individuals rather than systematic control

green.label: Effective / audit-ready
criteria: Control is documented, implemented, and enforced
Evidence is recent, repeatable, and preferably automated
Ownership, monitoring, and review cadence are in place

domains.name: Governance, Risk & Compliance
domains.description: Security governance, accountability, risk management, and formal GDPR compliance structures.

controls.name: Information Security Policy
controls.description: Maintain a versioned and approved security policy set.

scoring_criteria.red: No formal policy set, or policies are outdated and not approved.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Policies exist but are incomplete, stale, or not reviewed consistently.
scoring_criteria.green: Policies are versioned, approved, reviewed annually, and linked to controls.

controls.name: Risk Register
controls.description: Track material security, privacy, and compliance risks with owners and mitigation plans.

scoring_criteria.red: No active risk register or no clear risk ownership.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Risk register exists but is incomplete or not updated regularly.
scoring_criteria.green: Risk register is current, prioritized, owned, and reviewed on a schedule.

controls.name: RoPA and GDPR Accountability
controls.description: Maintain Records of Processing Activities and accountability documentation.

scoring_criteria.red: No RoPA or no clear record of processing activities.
scoring_criteria.yellow: RoPA exists but is partial, outdated, or not tied to real systems.
scoring_criteria.green: RoPA is current, complete, mapped to systems, and reviewed periodically.

controls.name: DPIA Process
controls.description: Perform DPIAs for high-risk processing activities.
evidence_examples: Decision log for when DPIA is required

scoring_criteria.red: No DPIA process or high-risk processing without assessment.
scoring_criteria.yellow: DPIAs are done inconsistently or only after launch.
scoring_criteria.green: DPIAs are required, documented, and completed before risky processing starts.

controls.name: Management Reporting
controls.description: Provide regular security and compliance reporting to leadership.

scoring_criteria.red: Leadership receives little or no formal security reporting.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reporting exists but is ad hoc or operationally shallow.
scoring_criteria.green: Leadership receives recurring, decision-useful reporting with trends and actions.

domains.name: Data Protection & Privacy
domains.description: GDPR-focused controls over customer and employee personal data.

controls.name: Data Inventory and Classification
controls.description: Maintain an inventory of systems, data categories, and classification levels.

scoring_criteria.red: No reliable inventory of personal data or data classes.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Inventory exists for key systems only or classification is inconsistent.
scoring_criteria.green: Inventory is comprehensive, classified, and linked to owners and retention.

controls.name: Lawful Basis and Purpose Limitation
controls.description: Document the lawful basis and specific purpose for each processing activity.

scoring_criteria.red: Lawful basis is unclear or undocumented for one or more processing activities.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Lawful basis is documented for some but not all core processing activities.
scoring_criteria.green: Each processing activity has a documented lawful basis and purpose, aligned with notices.

controls.name: Data Subject Rights Handling
controls.description: Support access, deletion, rectification, portability, and objection requests within deadlines.

scoring_criteria.red: No operational DSAR process or repeated missed deadlines.
scoring_criteria.yellow: DSARs are handled manually with inconsistent completeness.
scoring_criteria.green: DSAR workflow is defined, tracked, timely, and testable across systems.

controls.name: Retention and Deletion
controls.description: Enforce retention schedules and deletion for personal data.

scoring_criteria.red: No retention schedule or indefinite storage of personal data without justification.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Retention rules exist but are not automated or not enforced everywhere.
scoring_criteria.green: Retention rules are documented, justified, automated where possible, and evidenced.

controls.name: Encryption and Key Management
controls.description: Protect personal data in transit and at rest with appropriate cryptography.

scoring_criteria.red: Sensitive data is unencrypted in key systems or key management is weak.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Encryption is present in major systems but exceptions exist or keys are weakly managed.
scoring_criteria.green: Encryption is standardized, enforced, and keys are centrally managed and rotated.

controls.name: International Transfers
controls.description: Control and document transfers of personal data outside the EEA.

scoring_criteria.red: Cross-border transfers occur without clear safeguards or visibility.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Safeguards exist for major vendors but documentation is partial.
scoring_criteria.green: All relevant transfers are identified, justified, and supported by current safeguards.

domains.name: Payment Security (PCI DSS)
domains.description: Controls for payment data handling and PCI DSS scope minimization.

controls.name: PCI Scope Definition
controls.description: Clearly define and minimize the cardholder data environment.

scoring_criteria.red: PCI scope is unknown, poorly defined, or broader than necessary.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Scope is defined but has unresolved ambiguities or expansion risks.
scoring_criteria.green: Scope is current, minimized, approved, and supported by architecture evidence.

controls.name: No Raw Card Data Storage
controls.description: Do not store PAN, CVV, or other prohibited payment data internally unless required and protected.

scoring_criteria.red: Evidence suggests raw card data may be stored or the answer is unclear.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Policy says no storage, but verification is partial or infrequent.
scoring_criteria.green: Regular scans and architecture confirm prohibited card data is not stored.

controls.name: Use of PSP / Tokenization
controls.description: Use external payment providers and tokenization to reduce risk.
evidence_examples: Hosted fields or redirect implementation screenshots

scoring_criteria.red: Payment flow exposes internal systems directly to card data unnecessarily.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Third-party PSP is used but implementation still leaves avoidable risk.
scoring_criteria.green: Tokenized, low-scope payment flow is in place and documented.

controls.name: PCI Validation Evidence
controls.description: Maintain current PCI DSS evidence and validation artifacts.

scoring_criteria.red: No current PCI evidence or failed required validation activities.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some validation artifacts exist but are stale or incomplete.
scoring_criteria.green: Required PCI evidence is current, complete, and remediation is tracked.

domains.name: Identity & Access Management
domains.description: Access control, MFA, RBAC, reviews, and lifecycle management.

controls.name: MFA Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce MFA for privileged access and critical business systems.

scoring_criteria.red: No MFA for admins or key business systems.
scoring_criteria.yellow: MFA is enabled for some systems but not consistently enforced.
scoring_criteria.green: MFA is enforced for privileged users and critical systems with coverage evidence.

controls.name: SSO Coverage
controls.description: Use centralized SSO for workforce applications where practical.

scoring_criteria.red: Users rely on local credentials across many systems without central control.
scoring_criteria.yellow: SSO is used for core tools but not broadly rolled out.
scoring_criteria.green: Most workforce systems use SSO and exceptions are documented and reviewed.

controls.name: RBAC and Least Privilege
controls.description: Define roles and keep access aligned to job need.

scoring_criteria.red: Users have broad or unmanaged permissions; shared accounts may exist.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Roles exist but over-privileged access remains common.
scoring_criteria.green: Roles are defined, permissions are minimized, and exceptions are reviewed.

controls.name: Access Reviews
controls.description: Perform periodic reviews of sensitive access.

scoring_criteria.red: No regular access review process exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reviews happen but are inconsistent, incomplete, or not remediated quickly.
scoring_criteria.green: Periodic reviews are completed, approved, and remediation is evidenced.

controls.name: Joiner / Mover / Leaver Process
controls.description: Timely provisioning and deprovisioning integrated with HR events.

scoring_criteria.red: Accounts linger after departures or access changes are unmanaged.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Lifecycle process exists but depends heavily on manual follow-up.
scoring_criteria.green: Lifecycle events are prompt, controlled, and evidenced with audit logs.

domains.name: Application Security
domains.description: Secure development lifecycle and vulnerability management for software and APIs.

controls.name: Secure SDLC
controls.description: Integrate security requirements, review, and testing into development.
evidence_examples: Definition of done with security checks

scoring_criteria.red: No structured secure SDLC or security is purely reactive.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Secure SDLC exists on paper but is not consistently used.
scoring_criteria.green: Security controls are embedded in the SDLC and used across engineering teams.

controls.name: Code Review and Branch Controls
controls.description: Require peer review and protected branches for production code.

scoring_criteria.red: Direct commits to production branches or weak review practices are common.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reviews are expected but not technically enforced everywhere.
scoring_criteria.green: Protected branches and review requirements are enforced and monitored.

controls.name: SAST / DAST / SCA
controls.description: Run security scanning on code, dependencies, and running applications.

scoring_criteria.red: Security scanning is absent or produces unreviewed results.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Scanning exists but is limited, noisy, or easy to bypass.
scoring_criteria.green: Scanning is integrated into CI/CD with triage and remediation tracking.

controls.name: Secrets Management
controls.description: Keep secrets out of code and manage them securely.

scoring_criteria.red: Secrets appear in code, configs, or chat; no central secret management.
scoring_criteria.yellow: A secret manager exists but adoption is incomplete.
scoring_criteria.green: Secrets are centrally managed, scanned for leakage, and regularly rotated.

controls.name: API Security
controls.description: Protect APIs with authentication, authorization, validation, and abuse controls.

scoring_criteria.red: APIs have weak auth or no clear abuse protections.
scoring_criteria.yellow: API protections exist but coverage is uneven across services.
scoring_criteria.green: API standards are enforced with auth, validation, and rate limiting controls.

controls.name: Penetration Testing
controls.description: Conduct application and platform penetration tests and track remediation.

scoring_criteria.red: No recent penetration test for core customer-facing scope.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Testing exists but remediation or retesting is weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Relevant pentests are recent, scoped well, and findings are tracked to closure.

domains.name: Cloud & Infrastructure Security
domains.description: Secure cloud configuration, patching, segmentation, containers, and CI/CD.

controls.name: Cloud Configuration Monitoring
controls.description: Continuously detect and remediate cloud misconfigurations.

scoring_criteria.red: No reliable visibility into misconfiguration risk across cloud accounts.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Monitoring exists but coverage or remediation discipline is weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Cloud posture is continuously monitored and material issues are remediated.

controls.name: Network Segmentation
controls.description: Separate environments and sensitive systems with clear trust boundaries.

scoring_criteria.red: Flat or weakly segmented environments increase blast radius.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Segmentation exists but exceptions are numerous or poorly governed.
scoring_criteria.green: Segmentation is deliberate, documented, and supports least connectivity.

controls.name: Patch and Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Patch infrastructure and track remediation against SLAs.

scoring_criteria.red: No defined patching cadence or critical systems remain unpatched too long.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Patching occurs but compliance is inconsistent or poorly evidenced.
scoring_criteria.green: Patching is governed, measured, and exceptions are formally approved.

controls.name: Container and Image Security
controls.description: Scan images and manage runtime risk for containers.

scoring_criteria.red: Container use is unmanaged or images are not scanned.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Image scanning exists but runtime or remediation controls are weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Container lifecycle is secured with image scanning, policy, and monitoring.

controls.name: CI/CD Security
controls.description: Protect build and deployment systems from unauthorized or unsafe changes.

scoring_criteria.red: Deployment pipelines have weak access control or no integrity safeguards.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some controls exist but gaps remain in approval, provenance, or secret handling.
scoring_criteria.green: Pipelines are access-controlled, monitored, and include security gates.

domains.name: Monitoring, Logging & Detection
domains.description: Centralized logging, alerting, and detection capability.

controls.name: Centralized Logging
controls.description: Collect logs centrally from critical systems and retain them appropriately.

scoring_criteria.red: Logs are fragmented, missing, or unavailable for key systems.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Central logging exists but key sources or retention are incomplete.
scoring_criteria.green: Critical systems log centrally with defined retention and access controls.

controls.name: Audit Logging for Sensitive Actions
controls.description: Log administrative and sensitive data-access actions.

scoring_criteria.red: Sensitive actions are not reliably logged.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some sensitive activity is logged but not comprehensively.
scoring_criteria.green: Sensitive actions are logged, protected, and reviewable.

controls.name: Security Alerting and Triage
controls.description: Alert on suspicious behavior and route it for triage.

scoring_criteria.red: No meaningful security alerting or triage workflow exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Alerts exist but are noisy, incomplete, or inconsistently handled.
scoring_criteria.green: Alerting is tuned, owned, and linked to response procedures.

controls.name: Detection Metrics
controls.description: Measure and improve detection speed and signal quality.

scoring_criteria.red: No detection performance metrics are tracked.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some metrics exist but are not used to improve outcomes.
scoring_criteria.green: Detection metrics are tracked and used to improve alerting and response.

domains.name: Incident Response & Breach Management
domains.description: Preparedness to detect, contain, recover, and notify under GDPR timelines.

controls.name: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Maintain and socialize an actionable incident response plan.

scoring_criteria.red: No formal IR plan or major ambiguity during incidents.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Plan exists but is incomplete, stale, or not well understood.
scoring_criteria.green: Plan is current, accessible, and roles are clearly assigned.

controls.name: GDPR Breach Notification
controls.description: Be able to assess and notify within legal timeframes when required.

scoring_criteria.red: No formal breach notification process or timing cannot be met.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Procedure exists but legal and operational roles are not well integrated.
scoring_criteria.green: Notification workflow is defined, timed, and tested with legal/privacy involvement.

controls.name: Exercises and Tabletop Testing
controls.description: Run exercises to test preparedness and improve response.

scoring_criteria.red: No exercises have been run for core scenarios.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Exercises occur rarely or findings are not followed through.
scoring_criteria.green: Exercises are recurring, realistic, and drive measurable improvements.

controls.name: Backup and Restore Validation
controls.description: Backups exist, are protected, and restores are tested.

scoring_criteria.red: Backups are missing, unreliable, or never tested for restore.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Backups exist but restore testing is infrequent or incomplete.
scoring_criteria.green: Backup and restore processes are tested and meet stated recovery targets.

controls.name: Incident Register and Lessons Learned
controls.description: Track incidents and follow up with root-cause and improvement actions.

scoring_criteria.red: Incidents are not consistently recorded or reviewed.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Incident tracking exists but postmortems and follow-up are weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Incidents are recorded, reviewed, and linked to improvement actions.

domains.name: Third-Party & Processor Management
domains.description: Vendor due diligence, DPAs, subprocessors, and ongoing monitoring.

controls.name: Vendor Inventory
controls.description: Maintain an inventory of vendors, processors, and their data access.

scoring_criteria.red: No reliable list of vendors handling data or critical services.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Inventory exists but lacks data-access detail or risk classification.
scoring_criteria.green: Inventory is current, risk-tiered, and linked to owners and contracts.

controls.name: DPAs and Contractual Controls
controls.description: Ensure DPAs and security clauses are in place where required.

scoring_criteria.red: Required DPAs or privacy/security clauses are missing.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Core vendors are covered but some contracts are outdated or inconsistent.
scoring_criteria.green: Relevant contracts are current and aligned to data use and risk.

controls.name: Vendor Security Due Diligence
controls.description: Assess security posture of important vendors before and during use.

scoring_criteria.red: No structured vendor security review process exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reviews are done for some vendors but not consistently by risk tier.
scoring_criteria.green: Due diligence is risk-based, documented, and repeated on a schedule.

controls.name: Subprocessor Transparency and Transfers
controls.description: Track subprocessors and their transfer implications.

scoring_criteria.red: Subprocessors are not visible or not controlled.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Subprocessor information exists but is incomplete or not maintained.
scoring_criteria.green: Subprocessors are documented, reviewed, and linked to transfer safeguards.

domains.name: eCommerce-Specific Security
domains.description: Fraud, bots, account takeover, checkout abuse, and business logic risks.

controls.name: Bot and Abuse Protection
controls.description: Protect login, search, checkout, and promo flows from automated abuse.

scoring_criteria.red: Public critical flows lack meaningful bot or abuse protections.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Basic protections exist but are not tuned or not applied consistently.
scoring_criteria.green: Critical flows have layered bot, WAF, and rate-limiting defenses.

controls.name: Account Takeover Defenses
controls.description: Detect and prevent credential stuffing and suspicious account activity.

scoring_criteria.red: ATO risk is unmanaged or detection is absent.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some controls exist but response and tuning are immature.
scoring_criteria.green: ATO controls are in place with monitoring, tuning, and response workflows.

controls.name: Fraud Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor card testing, chargebacks, refund abuse, and other transaction fraud.

scoring_criteria.red: No clear fraud monitoring or ownership exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Fraud controls are reactive and depend heavily on manual review.
scoring_criteria.green: Fraud rules, reporting, and ownership are established and measured.

controls.name: Pricing / Promo / Order Integrity
controls.description: Prevent abuse of business logic, promotions, and order flows.
evidence_examples: Admin audit logs for price changes

scoring_criteria.red: Key commercial controls are weak and susceptible to manipulation.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Controls exist but are inconsistent or not well audited.
scoring_criteria.green: Integrity controls are defined, logged, and tested against abuse scenarios.

controls.name: Checkout Security Design
controls.description: Maintain a secure and resilient checkout architecture.

scoring_criteria.red: Checkout security posture is poorly understood or fragile.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Checkout design is documented but some security dependencies are weakly controlled.
scoring_criteria.green: Checkout architecture is documented, reviewed, and resilient under abuse and failure.

domains.name: Security Culture & Organization
domains.description: Training, ownership, and security operating model.

controls.name: Security Awareness Training
controls.description: Train staff on security, privacy, and phishing risk.

scoring_criteria.red: No meaningful awareness training program exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Training exists but completion, recency, or coverage is weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Training is recurring, measured, and includes targeted follow-up.

controls.name: Secure Coding Training
controls.description: Train engineers on secure development practices.

scoring_criteria.red: Engineering receives no structured secure coding training.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Training exists but is informal or not regularly refreshed.
scoring_criteria.green: Role-appropriate training is completed and refreshed on a schedule.

controls.name: Security Ownership Model
controls.description: Define security responsibilities across teams and champions.

scoring_criteria.red: Security responsibilities are unclear or centralized without support.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Ownership is partly defined but uneven across teams.
scoring_criteria.green: Responsibilities are clear and reinforced through team-level ownership.

domains.name: Legal, Transparency & Cookie Compliance
domains.description: External statements, cookies, terms, and actual practice alignment.

controls.name: Privacy Notice Accuracy
controls.description: Ensure privacy notices accurately reflect actual processing.

scoring_criteria.red: Public privacy statements materially differ from actual practice.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Privacy notice is mostly accurate but some processing or wording is outdated.
scoring_criteria.green: Privacy notices are current, specific, and mapped to actual processing.

controls.name: Cookie and Consent Compliance
controls.description: Control cookies and consent behavior in line with EU requirements.

scoring_criteria.red: Non-essential cookies fire without valid consent or visibility is poor.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Consent tooling exists but scans or categories are incomplete.
scoring_criteria.green: Cookie controls are configured correctly and supported by current scans and logs.

controls.name: Terms and Data Clauses
controls.description: Align customer and partner contracts with actual security and data practices.

scoring_criteria.red: Contractual commitments do not reflect actual operational capability.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Key clauses exist but are not consistently aligned or reviewed.
scoring_criteria.green: Legal commitments are reviewed and aligned with operational practice.

domains.name: Metrics, Audit & Continuous Improvement
domains.description: Use KPIs, audits, and remediation tracking to improve over time.

controls.name: Security KPI Dashboard
controls.description: Track a core set of measurable security and compliance indicators.

scoring_criteria.red: No agreed security KPI set exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Some metrics are tracked but lack consistency or audience relevance.
scoring_criteria.green: KPIs are defined, trended, and used in management decision-making.

controls.name: Vulnerability Remediation SLAs
controls.description: Define and measure remediation performance by severity.

scoring_criteria.red: No remediation SLAs or widespread breach of expectations.
scoring_criteria.yellow: SLAs exist but performance or exception handling is weak.
scoring_criteria.green: SLAs are measurable, monitored, and exceptions are controlled.

controls.name: Audit Findings Tracking
controls.description: Track audit and assessment findings to closure.

scoring_criteria.red: Findings are not centrally tracked or close-out is unreliable.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Tracking exists but ownership or timeliness is weak.
scoring_criteria.green: Findings are tracked with owners, due dates, and closure evidence.

controls.name: Periodic Internal Review
controls.description: Review the control environment on a recurring cadence.

scoring_criteria.red: No recurring review cadence exists.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reviews happen but lack structure or completeness.
scoring_criteria.green: Reviews are scheduled, evidence-based, and drive updates.

domains.name: EU Digital Services Act (DSA) & Digital Markets Act (DMA)
domains.description: Compliance with EU platform regulation covering transparency, illegal content handling, marketplace accountability, and fair competition obligations.

controls.name: Notice and Action Mechanism
controls.description: Provide mechanisms for users and authorities to report illegal content and act on it.

scoring_criteria.red: No clear reporting mechanism or no action process.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Reporting exists but handling is inconsistent or slow.
scoring_criteria.green: Well-defined, timely notice-and-action process with audit logs.

controls.name: Content Moderation Transparency
controls.description: Document and report moderation decisions and policies.

scoring_criteria.red: No transparency on moderation decisions.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Partial documentation without consistent reporting.
scoring_criteria.green: Regular transparency reporting and traceable moderation decisions.

controls.name: Trader / Seller Verification (Marketplace)
controls.description: Verify identity of sellers on the platform (Know Your Business Customer).

scoring_criteria.red: No seller verification process.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Basic verification without strong validation.
scoring_criteria.green: Robust verification with audit trail and periodic review.

controls.name: Recommendation System Transparency
controls.description: Explain how recommendation or ranking systems work.

scoring_criteria.red: No visibility into recommendation logic.
scoring_criteria.yellow: High-level explanation without user control.
scoring_criteria.green: Clear explanation and user control options provided.

controls.name: Fair Platform Practices
controls.description: Ensure no unfair self-preferencing or anti-competitive practices.

scoring_criteria.red: Potential anti-competitive practices with no oversight.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Policies exist but no active monitoring.
scoring_criteria.green: Active monitoring and documented compliance with fair practices.

controls.name: Data Access and Portability
controls.description: Provide business users access to their data and portability options.

scoring_criteria.red: No data access or export capability.
scoring_criteria.yellow: Partial access with limitations.
scoring_criteria.green: Full, documented, and tested data portability mechanisms.
